title: Classic Spaghetti Carbonara
ingredients: 200g spaghetti, 100g pancetta, 2 large eggs, 50g Pecorino Romano, 50g Parmesan, 2 cloves garlic, black pepper, salt
instructions:
1. Cook spaghetti in salted boiling water until al dente.
2. Fry pancetta with garlic until crispy.
3. Whisk eggs with grated Pecorino and Parmesan.
4. Remove pan from heat, add drained pasta, toss.
5. Pour egg mixture over pasta, toss vigorously off heat.
6. Season generously with black pepper and serve immediately.

title: Lemon Herb Roasted Chicken
ingredients: 1 whole chicken (1.5kg), 2 lemons, 4 cloves garlic, fresh rosemary, fresh thyme, 3 tbsp olive oil, salt, black pepper, 1 onion
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F).
2. Rub chicken with olive oil, salt and pepper inside and out.
3. Stuff cavity with lemon halves, garlic, and fresh herbs.
4. Place on a bed of sliced onion.
5. Roast for 80 minutes, basting every 25 minutes.
6. Rest 10 minutes before carving.

title: Mango Avocado Salsa
ingredients: 2 ripe mangoes, 2 avocados, 1 red onion, 1 jalapeño, juice of 2 limes, fresh cilantro, salt, pinch of cayenne
instructions:
1. Dice mangoes and avocados into small cubes.
2. Finely chop red onion and jalapeño (seeds removed).
3. Combine all in a bowl.
4. Add lime juice, chopped cilantro, salt, and cayenne.
5. Gently toss and refrigerate for 15 minutes before serving.
6. Great with tortilla chips or as a fish taco topping.

title: Chocolate Lava Cake
ingredients: 200g dark chocolate, 100g butter, 4 eggs, 4 egg yolks, 150g caster sugar, 50g plain flour, cocoa powder for dusting
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Butter and dust 4 ramekins with cocoa.
2. Melt chocolate and butter together until smooth.
3. Whisk eggs, yolks, and sugar until pale and thick.
4. Fold chocolate into egg mixture, then fold in flour.
5. Divide into ramekins, refrigerate up to 24hrs or bake immediately.
6. Bake 12 minutes — edges firm, center jiggly. Invert and serve at once.

title: Thai Green Curry
ingredients: 400ml coconut milk, 3 tbsp green curry paste, 500g chicken breast, 2 zucchinis, 1 red bell pepper, fish sauce, palm sugar, Thai basil, jasmine rice
instructions:
1. Heat 1 tbsp coconut milk in wok until it splits.
2. Fry curry paste for 2 minutes until fragrant.
3. Add chicken, stir-fry until sealed.
4. Pour in remaining coconut milk, bring to simmer.
5. Add vegetables, fish sauce, and palm sugar to taste.
6. Cook 10 minutes, finish with Thai basil. Serve over jasmine rice.

title: Blueberry Lemon Pancakes
ingredients: 1½ cups flour, 2 tsp baking powder, 1 tbsp sugar, 1 egg, 1 cup milk, zest of 1 lemon, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 cup blueberries, 2 tbsp butter, pinch of salt
instructions:
1. Whisk flour, baking powder, sugar, and salt in a bowl.
2. In another bowl, whisk egg, milk, lemon zest, and juice.
3. Combine wet and dry ingredients until just mixed (lumps are fine).
4. Fold in blueberries gently.
5. Heat buttered skillet over medium. Pour ¼ cup batter per pancake.
6. Cook until bubbles form, flip once, cook 1 more minute. Serve with syrup.

title: Classic French Toast
ingredients: 4 thick slices brioche, 2 eggs, ½ cup milk, 1 tsp vanilla extract, 1 tsp cinnamon, 2 tbsp butter, maple syrup, powdered sugar, fresh berries
instructions:
1. Whisk eggs, milk, vanilla, and cinnamon in a shallow dish.
2. Dip brioche slices for 30 seconds each side to soak.
3. Melt butter in skillet over medium heat.
4. Cook soaked slices 2–3 minutes per side until golden.
5. Serve with maple syrup, powdered sugar, and fresh berries.

title: Avocado Toast with Poached Egg
ingredients: 2 slices sourdough, 1 ripe avocado, 2 eggs, 1 tbsp white vinegar, lemon juice, chili flakes, salt, pepper, microgreens
instructions:
1. Toast sourdough until golden and crispy.
2. Mash avocado with lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
3. Bring a pot of water with vinegar to gentle simmer.
4. Swirl water, crack in egg, poach for 3 minutes.
5. Spread avocado on toast, top with poached egg.
6. Finish with chili flakes and microgreens.

title: Banana Overnight Oats
ingredients: ½ cup rolled oats, ½ cup milk, ¼ cup Greek yogurt, 1 ripe banana, 1 tbsp chia seeds, 1 tbsp honey, ½ tsp vanilla, pinch of cinnamon
instructions:
1. Mash banana with a fork in a jar or bowl.
2. Add oats, milk, yogurt, chia seeds, honey, vanilla, and cinnamon.
3. Stir everything together until well combined.
4. Cover and refrigerate overnight (at least 6 hours).
5. In the morning, top with sliced banana, nuts, or granola and enjoy cold.

title: Eggs Benedict
ingredients: 2 English muffins, 4 eggs, 4 slices Canadian bacon, 3 egg yolks, 115g butter, 1 tbsp lemon juice, white vinegar, cayenne, salt
instructions:
1. Make hollandaise: whisk yolks over bain-marie until thick. Slowly whisk in melted butter. Add lemon juice, cayenne, salt.
2. Toast muffins and warm bacon in a pan.
3. Poach eggs in simmering vinegared water for 3 minutes.
4. Stack: muffin half → bacon → poached egg → hollandaise.
5. Dust with cayenne and serve immediately.

title: Acai Smoothie Bowl
ingredients: 200g frozen acai puree, 1 frozen banana, ½ cup coconut milk, toppings: granola, kiwi, strawberries, coconut flakes, honey, chia seeds
instructions:
1. Blend frozen acai, banana, and coconut milk until thick and smooth.
2. Pour into a bowl — it should be thick enough to hold toppings.
3. Arrange granola, sliced kiwi, strawberries, and coconut flakes.
4. Drizzle with honey and sprinkle chia seeds.
5. Serve immediately before it melts.

title: Crispy Bruschetta
ingredients: 1 baguette, 4 ripe tomatoes, 3 cloves garlic, fresh basil, 3 tbsp extra virgin olive oil, balsamic glaze, salt, black pepper
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 200°C. Slice baguette diagonally and toast until golden.
2. Rub each slice with a halved garlic clove.
3. Dice tomatoes and combine with chopped basil, olive oil, salt, and pepper.
4. Let mixture rest 10 minutes for flavors to meld.
5. Spoon tomato mixture onto toasts.
6. Drizzle with balsamic glaze and serve immediately.

title: Crispy Spring Rolls
ingredients: 12 spring roll wrappers, 200g ground pork, 1 cup shredded cabbage, 1 carrot (grated), 2 green onions, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp sesame oil, garlic, ginger, oil for frying
instructions:
1. Stir-fry pork with garlic and ginger until cooked.
2. Add cabbage, carrot, green onions, soy sauce, sesame oil. Cook 3 minutes.
3. Cool filling completely.
4. Place 2 tbsp filling on each wrapper, fold sides in, roll tightly.
5. Seal edge with water. Deep fry at 175°C for 3–4 minutes until golden.
6. Drain and serve with sweet chili dipping sauce.

title: Stuffed Mushrooms
ingredients: 16 large cremini mushrooms, 200g cream cheese, ½ cup breadcrumbs, 3 cloves garlic, ¼ cup Parmesan, 2 tbsp fresh parsley, 2 tbsp olive oil, salt, pepper
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 190°C. Remove mushroom stems and chop finely.
2. Sauté chopped stems with garlic in olive oil for 5 minutes.
3. Mix with cream cheese, breadcrumbs, Parmesan, parsley, salt, pepper.
4. Fill each mushroom cap with the mixture, mounding generously.
5. Bake 20 minutes until tops are golden and mushrooms are tender.

title: Caprese Skewers
ingredients: 250g fresh mozzarella balls, 250g cherry tomatoes, fresh basil leaves, balsamic glaze, extra virgin olive oil, sea salt, black pepper
instructions:
1. Thread tomato, basil leaf, and mozzarella ball alternately onto skewers.
2. Arrange on a platter.
3. Drizzle generously with olive oil and balsamic glaze.
4. Season with sea salt and freshly cracked black pepper.
5. Serve immediately or refrigerate up to 2 hours before serving.

title: Classic Hummus
ingredients: 400g canned chickpeas, 3 tbsp tahini, 2 cloves garlic, juice of 1 lemon, 3 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp cumin, ½ tsp salt, ice water, paprika for garnish
instructions:
1. Drain and rinse chickpeas. Save some for garnish.
2. Blend chickpeas, tahini, garlic, lemon juice, cumin, and salt until smooth.
3. With blender running, drizzle in olive oil and 2–3 tbsp ice water.
4. Blend 3 minutes until very smooth and creamy.
5. Transfer to bowl, swirl top, drizzle olive oil, dust with paprika.
6. Serve with pita, cucumbers, and carrots.

title: Caesar Salad
ingredients: 2 romaine hearts, 1 cup croutons, 80g Parmesan (shaved), 2 egg yolks, 2 cloves garlic, 4 anchovy fillets, 1 tbsp Dijon mustard, juice of 1 lemon, 1 tsp Worcestershire, ½ cup olive oil
instructions:
1. Make dressing: blend yolks, garlic, anchovies, mustard, lemon, Worcestershire.
2. Slowly drizzle in olive oil while blending until emulsified.
3. Season dressing with salt and pepper.
4. Tear romaine into bite-sized pieces.
5. Toss with dressing until well coated.
6. Top with croutons and shaved Parmesan. Serve immediately.

title: Greek Salad
ingredients: 3 tomatoes, 1 cucumber, 1 red onion, 200g feta cheese, 1 cup kalamata olives, 1 green bell pepper, 4 tbsp olive oil, 2 tbsp red wine vinegar, 1 tsp dried oregano
instructions:
1. Chop tomatoes, cucumber, and bell pepper into large chunks.
2. Thinly slice red onion.
3. Combine all vegetables and olives in a bowl.
4. Place feta block on top (or crumble over).
5. Whisk olive oil and vinegar, drizzle over salad.
6. Sprinkle oregano and serve with crusty bread.

title: Warm Quinoa & Roasted Veggie Salad
ingredients: 1½ cups quinoa, 1 sweet potato, 1 zucchini, 1 red pepper, 1 red onion, 80g rocket, 50g feta, 3 tbsp olive oil, 2 tbsp balsamic, 1 tsp cumin, salt, pepper
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 200°C. Cube sweet potato, dice zucchini, pepper and onion.
2. Toss vegetables with olive oil, cumin, salt and roast 25 minutes.
3. Rinse quinoa and cook in 3 cups water for 15 minutes. Drain.
4. Make dressing: whisk balsamic, olive oil, salt, pepper.
5. Combine warm quinoa, roasted vegetables, and rocket.
6. Drizzle dressing, crumble feta on top and serve warm.

title: Waldorf Salad
ingredients: 2 apples, 3 celery stalks, 1 cup grapes (halved), ½ cup walnuts, ½ cup mayonnaise, 2 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tsp honey, romaine lettuce, salt, pepper
instructions:
1. Core and dice apples, toss immediately with lemon juice.
2. Slice celery thinly. Halve grapes.
3. Toast walnuts in a dry pan 3–4 minutes until fragrant.
4. Mix mayonnaise, honey, remaining lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
5. Combine apples, celery, grapes, and dressing. Toss gently.
6. Serve on a bed of lettuce, topped with toasted walnuts.

title: Thai Papaya Salad
ingredients: 1 green papaya, 2 cloves garlic, 2 bird's eye chilies, 1 tbsp fish sauce, 1 tbsp lime juice, 1 tsp palm sugar, 60g cherry tomatoes, 30g roasted peanuts, dried shrimp (optional)
instructions:
1. Peel and shred green papaya into long thin strips.
2. In a mortar, pound garlic and chilies to a paste.
3. Add palm sugar, fish sauce, and lime juice. Pound to combine.
4. Add cherry tomatoes, lightly pound to bruise them.
5. Add papaya shreds and toss everything together.
6. Top with roasted peanuts and serve immediately.

title: Pesto Pasta
ingredients: 400g trofie or linguine, 60g fresh basil, 50g Parmesan (grated), 30g pine nuts, 2 cloves garlic, 80ml extra virgin olive oil, salt, black pepper
instructions:
1. Cook pasta in salted boiling water until al dente.
2. Toast pine nuts in a dry pan until golden (2 minutes).
3. Blend basil, Parmesan, pine nuts, and garlic. Season well.
4. Gradually drizzle in olive oil while blending until smooth.
5. Reserve ½ cup pasta water before draining.
6. Toss drained pasta with pesto, adding pasta water to loosen. Serve.

title: Cacio e Pepe
ingredients: 200g tonnarelli or spaghetti, 100g Pecorino Romano (finely grated), 50g Parmesan (finely grated), 2 tsp black pepper (coarsely cracked), salt, pasta water
instructions:
1. Cook pasta in generously salted water until just under al dente.
2. Toast cracked pepper in a wide pan for 1 minute.
3. Add a ladle of pasta water to the pan.
4. Add pasta directly to pan, toss vigorously off heat.
5. Add cheese gradually while tossing — the sauce should be creamy.
6. Add pasta water as needed. Serve immediately.

title: Beef Lasagna
ingredients: 12 lasagna sheets, 500g ground beef, 400g canned tomatoes, 1 onion, 3 cloves garlic, 500ml béchamel sauce, 150g mozzarella, 50g Parmesan, 2 tbsp olive oil, basil, oregano, salt, pepper
instructions:
1. Brown beef with onion and garlic. Add tomatoes, herbs, salt, pepper. Simmer 30 min.
2. Make béchamel: melt butter, whisk in flour, add warm milk gradually until thick.
3. Layer: beef ragu → lasagna sheets → béchamel. Repeat 3 times.
4. Top with béchamel, mozzarella, and Parmesan.
5. Cover with foil and bake at 180°C for 40 min.
6. Remove foil, bake 15 more min until bubbly and golden.

title: Aglio e Olio
ingredients: 200g spaghetti, 6 cloves garlic (thinly sliced), 60ml extra virgin olive oil, ½ tsp red pepper flakes, 2 tbsp fresh parsley, 30g Parmesan, salt
instructions:
1. Cook spaghetti in salted water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water.
2. Heat olive oil in a wide pan over medium-low heat.
3. Add garlic slices — cook slowly until golden (not burnt), about 4 minutes.
4. Add red pepper flakes, cook 30 seconds.
5. Add drained pasta and ¼ cup pasta water. Toss vigorously.
6. Add parsley and Parmesan. Toss and serve.

title: Homemade Mac and Cheese
ingredients: 400g elbow macaroni, 50g butter, 3 tbsp plain flour, 2½ cups whole milk, 200g sharp cheddar, 100g Gruyère, 1 tsp Dijon mustard, ½ tsp paprika, salt, white pepper, breadcrumbs for topping
instructions:
1. Cook macaroni until al dente. Drain and set aside.
2. Melt butter in a saucepan. Whisk in flour, cook 1 minute.
3. Gradually add milk, whisking constantly until thick (5 min).
4. Remove from heat. Stir in cheeses, mustard, paprika, salt, pepper.
5. Fold in macaroni. Transfer to baking dish.
6. Top with breadcrumbs and bake at 190°C for 20 minutes until golden.

title: Pan-Seared Salmon
ingredients: 2 salmon fillets (skin on), 1 tbsp olive oil, 2 tbsp butter, 2 cloves garlic, fresh dill, lemon, capers, salt, pepper
instructions:
1. Pat salmon dry and season generously with salt and pepper.
2. Heat olive oil in a skillet over high heat.
3. Place salmon skin-side down. Cook 4 minutes without touching.
4. Flip, reduce heat to medium. Add butter, garlic, and baste fish.
5. Cook 3 more minutes until just cooked through.
6. Serve with lemon wedges, fresh dill, and capers.

title: Beef Tacos
ingredients: 500g ground beef, 8 corn tortillas, 1 onion, 3 cloves garlic, 1 tbsp cumin, 1 tbsp chili powder, 1 tsp paprika, salt, lime, avocado, salsa, sour cream, shredded cheddar, cilantro
instructions:
1. Brown beef with diced onion and garlic over high heat.
2. Season with cumin, chili powder, paprika, and salt.
3. Add a splash of water and simmer 5 minutes.
4. Warm tortillas in a dry skillet or directly over flame.
5. Fill tortillas with beef, top with salsa, avocado, sour cream, and cheese.
6. Squeeze lime juice over and garnish with cilantro.

title: Lamb Chops with Mint
ingredients: 8 lamb chops, 3 cloves garlic, 2 tbsp rosemary, 2 tbsp olive oil, 1 cup fresh mint, ¼ cup white wine vinegar, 1 tsp sugar, salt, pepper
instructions:
1. Marinate chops in garlic, rosemary, olive oil, salt, and pepper for 1 hour.
2. Make mint sauce: blend mint, vinegar, and sugar. Season to taste.
3. Bring chops to room temperature 30 min before cooking.
4. Heat a grill pan over high heat. Cook chops 3 minutes per side for medium-rare.
5. Rest under foil for 5 minutes.
6. Serve with mint sauce and roasted potatoes.

title: Honey Garlic Shrimp Stir Fry
ingredients: 500g large shrimp (peeled), 4 cloves garlic, 3 tbsp honey, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp sesame oil, 1 tbsp cornstarch, 2 broccoli heads, 1 red pepper, green onions, sesame seeds, rice to serve
instructions:
1. Mix honey, soy sauce, sesame oil, and cornstarch for sauce.
2. Season shrimp with salt and pepper.
3. Stir-fry shrimp in oil over high heat 1–2 minutes per side.
4. Remove shrimp. In same pan stir-fry broccoli and pepper 3 minutes.
5. Return shrimp, pour in sauce. Toss until everything is coated and glossy.
6. Serve over rice, topped with green onions and sesame seeds.

title: Vegetable Masala Curry
ingredients: 1 cauliflower, 2 potatoes, 1 can chickpeas, 1 can coconut milk, 1 onion, 4 cloves garlic, 1 tsp ginger, 2 tbsp garam masala, 1 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp cumin, canned tomatoes, fresh cilantro, naan to serve
instructions:
1. Sauté onion until golden. Add garlic and ginger paste.
2. Add spices and cook 1 minute until fragrant.
3. Add chopped tomatoes, cook 5 minutes.
4. Add cauliflower, potato, and chickpeas. Stir to coat.
5. Pour in coconut milk, simmer 25 minutes until vegetables are tender.
6. Finish with cilantro and serve with warm naan.

title: BBQ Pork Ribs
ingredients: 1.5kg pork ribs, 3 tbsp brown sugar, 2 tsp smoked paprika, 1 tsp garlic powder, 1 tsp onion powder, 1 tsp cayenne, 1 tsp cumin, salt, pepper, 1 cup BBQ sauce
instructions:
1. Mix all dry spices into a rub. Apply generously to ribs.
2. Refrigerate 1 hour (or overnight).
3. Preheat oven to 150°C. Wrap ribs tightly in foil.
4. Bake low and slow for 2.5 hours until tender.
5. Unwrap, brush generously with BBQ sauce.
6. Grill or broil for 10 minutes until edges are caramelized.

title: Beer-Battered Fish and Chips
ingredients: 4 cod or haddock fillets, 1 cup plain flour, 1 cup cold beer, 1 tsp baking powder, salt, pepper, 1kg potatoes (cut into chips), oil for frying, tartar sauce, malt vinegar
instructions:
1. Cut potatoes into thick chips. Blanch in boiling water 5 minutes, drain and dry.
2. Whisk flour, baking powder, salt, and cold beer until smooth.
3. Fry chips at 160°C for 5 minutes, drain. Increase oil to 190°C.
4. Dip fish in batter, letting excess drip off. Fry 4–5 minutes until golden.
5. Return chips to 190°C oil for 2 minutes to crisp.
6. Serve with tartar sauce and malt vinegar.

title: Mushroom Risotto
ingredients: 350g Arborio rice, 300g mixed mushrooms, 1 onion, 2 cloves garlic, 150ml white wine, 1.2L hot chicken stock, 50g Parmesan, 50g butter, 2 tbsp olive oil, fresh thyme, salt, pepper
instructions:
1. Sauté mushrooms in butter until golden. Set aside.
2. In same pan, cook onion and garlic in olive oil until soft.
3. Add rice, toast 1 minute. Pour in wine, stir until absorbed.
4. Add hot stock one ladle at a time, stirring constantly.
5. Continue 20 minutes until rice is creamy and al dente.
6. Stir in Parmesan, butter, and mushrooms. Season and serve.

title: Garlic Butter Bread
ingredients: 1 French baguette, 100g butter (softened), 4 cloves garlic (minced), 2 tbsp fresh parsley, ¼ tsp dried oregano, pinch of salt
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 200°C.
2. Mix softened butter with garlic, parsley, oregano, and salt.
3. Slice baguette diagonally (without cutting all the way through).
4. Spread garlic butter generously between each slice.
5. Wrap in foil and bake 10 minutes.
6. Open foil, bake 5 more minutes until edges are golden and crispy.

title: Honey Roasted Vegetables
ingredients: 2 carrots, 2 parsnips, 1 red onion, 1 sweet potato, 1 zucchini, 3 tbsp olive oil, 2 tbsp honey, 1 tbsp balsamic vinegar, fresh thyme, rosemary, salt, pepper
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 200°C. Chop all vegetables into similar-sized chunks.
2. Toss with olive oil, honey, balsamic, thyme, rosemary, salt, and pepper.
3. Spread in a single layer on two baking sheets.
4. Roast 30–35 minutes until caramelized and tender, turning halfway.
5. Taste and adjust seasoning.
6. Garnish with fresh herbs and serve.

title: Creamy Mashed Potatoes
ingredients: 1.2kg Maris Piper potatoes, 100g butter, 150ml warm cream, 2 cloves garlic, salt, white pepper, chives to garnish
instructions:
1. Peel and cube potatoes. Cook in salted boiling water until very tender.
2. Roast garlic cloves in a little oil until golden and soft.
3. Drain potatoes thoroughly. Return to pot over low heat to steam dry.
4. Mash with a potato ricer or masher.
5. Fold in butter, then warm cream, then garlic. Season with salt and white pepper.
6. Beat until smooth and silky. Garnish with chives.

title: Classic Coleslaw
ingredients: ½ green cabbage, ¼ red cabbage, 2 carrots, ½ cup mayonnaise, 2 tbsp apple cider vinegar, 1 tbsp sugar, 1 tsp celery seeds, salt, pepper
instructions:
1. Thinly shred both cabbages using a mandoline or sharp knife.
2. Grate carrots on the coarse side of a box grater.
3. Whisk together mayo, vinegar, sugar, celery seeds, salt, and pepper.
4. Combine cabbage and carrot. Pour dressing over and toss well.
5. Taste and adjust sweetness, acidity, and salt.
6. Refrigerate at least 30 minutes before serving for best flavor.

title: Tomato Basil Soup
ingredients: 1kg ripe tomatoes, 1 onion, 4 cloves garlic, 400ml vegetable stock, 200ml cream, 2 tbsp olive oil, 1 bunch fresh basil, 1 tsp sugar, salt, pepper, crusty bread to serve
instructions:
1. Halve tomatoes and roast at 200°C with garlic and onion for 25 minutes.
2. Transfer roasted vegetables to a pot.
3. Add stock and basil. Bring to simmer for 5 minutes.
4. Blend until very smooth. Strain through a sieve for silky texture.
5. Stir in cream, sugar, salt, and pepper.
6. Reheat gently and serve with crusty bread and a drizzle of cream.

title: French Onion Soup
ingredients: 4 large onions, 50g butter, 1 tbsp olive oil, 200ml dry white wine, 1L beef stock, 1 tsp fresh thyme, 1 baguette, 150g Gruyère cheese, salt, pepper, bay leaf
instructions:
1. Caramelize onions in butter and oil over low heat for 45 minutes, stirring often.
2. Add wine, increase heat. Cook until wine evaporates.
3. Add stock, thyme, and bay leaf. Simmer 20 minutes. Season.
4. Slice baguette and toast slices.
5. Ladle soup into oven-safe bowls. Float toast on top, pile with Gruyère.
6. Broil 3–5 minutes until cheese is bubbling and golden.

title: Minestrone
ingredients: 1 onion, 3 cloves garlic, 2 carrots, 2 celery stalks, 2 zucchinis, 400g canned tomatoes, 400g white beans, 100g small pasta, 1.5L vegetable stock, 2 tbsp olive oil, basil, Parmesan rind, salt, pepper
instructions:
1. Sauté onion, carrot, and celery in olive oil until softened (8 min).
2. Add garlic and zucchini, cook 3 minutes.
3. Add tomatoes, stock, beans, and Parmesan rind. Season.
4. Simmer 20 minutes.
5. Add pasta, cook until al dente (10 min).
6. Remove rind. Stir in fresh basil. Serve with Parmesan and crusty bread.

title: Tonkotsu Ramen
ingredients: 1kg pork bones, 4 portions ramen noodles, 4 soft-boiled eggs, 200g chashu pork, 4 tbsp white miso, 2 tbsp soy sauce, sesame oil, green onions, nori, garlic, ginger
instructions:
1. Boil pork bones for 10 minutes. Drain, rinse, then simmer in fresh water 2 hours.
2. Strain broth. Season with miso, soy sauce, and sesame oil.
3. Cook chashu: roll pork belly, tie, sear all sides, braise in soy, mirin, sugar 1 hour.
4. Marinate peeled soft-boiled eggs in soy and mirin overnight.
5. Cook noodles per package. Divide into bowls.
6. Pour hot broth over. Top with chashu, egg, nori, and green onions.

title: New England Clam Chowder
ingredients: 500g clams (canned or fresh), 200g bacon (diced), 1 onion, 2 celery stalks, 3 potatoes, 2 cups cream, 1 cup clam juice, 2 tbsp butter, 2 tbsp flour, thyme, bay leaf, salt, pepper, fresh parsley
instructions:
1. Cook bacon until crispy. Remove, leaving fat in pot.
2. Sauté onion and celery in fat until soft. Add flour, cook 1 minute.
3. Pour in clam juice and cream gradually, whisking.
4. Add diced potatoes, thyme, and bay leaf. Simmer 20 minutes.
5. Add clams, heat through (don't overcook). Season.
6. Ladle into bowls, top with bacon and parsley.

title: Classic Tiramisu
ingredients: 500g mascarpone, 6 eggs (separated), 120g caster sugar, 300ml strong espresso (cooled), 24 savoiardi biscuits, 4 tbsp Marsala wine, cocoa powder for dusting
instructions:
1. Whisk yolks and sugar until pale and ribbon-like.
2. Fold in mascarpone until smooth.
3. Whisk egg whites to stiff peaks. Fold into mascarpone mixture in thirds.
4. Mix espresso with Marsala. Quickly dip each biscuit and layer in dish.
5. Spread half the cream over biscuits. Repeat layers.
6. Dust with cocoa, cover, and refrigerate at least 4 hours (overnight is best).

title: Crème Brûlée
ingredients: 500ml double cream, 6 egg yolks, 100g caster sugar, 1 vanilla pod, 4 tbsp caster sugar (for brûlée)
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 150°C. Split vanilla pod and scrape seeds into cream.
2. Heat cream to just below simmering. Remove vanilla pod.
3. Whisk yolks and sugar until combined. Slowly pour cream in, whisking.
4. Strain into ramekins placed in a roasting pan.
5. Fill pan with boiling water to halfway up ramekins. Bake 40 minutes.
6. Cool, refrigerate 2 hours. Sprinkle sugar on top and blowtorch until caramelized.

title: New York Cheesecake
ingredients: 250g digestive biscuits, 100g melted butter, 900g cream cheese, 200g caster sugar, 3 tbsp plain flour, 4 eggs, 1 egg yolk, 200ml sour cream, 1 tsp vanilla extract, zest of 1 lemon
instructions:
1. Crush biscuits, mix with butter, press into a 23cm springform pan. Chill.
2. Beat cream cheese and sugar until smooth.
3. Add flour, mix. Add eggs and yolk one at a time.
4. Stir in sour cream, vanilla, and lemon zest.
5. Pour onto base. Bake at 160°C in a water bath for 1 hour.
6. Cool in oven with door ajar 1 hour. Refrigerate overnight before slicing.

title: Classic Apple Pie
ingredients: 2 shortcrust pastry rounds, 1.2kg Bramley apples, 120g caster sugar, 2 tsp cinnamon, ½ tsp nutmeg, 1 tbsp cornstarch, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 egg (for wash), 2 tbsp butter
instructions:
1. Peel, core, and slice apples. Toss with sugar, cinnamon, nutmeg, cornstarch, and lemon juice.
2. Line a 23cm pie dish with one pastry round. Fill with apple mixture. Dot with butter.
3. Top with second pastry round. Crimp edges to seal.
4. Cut vents, brush with egg wash. Sprinkle sugar.
5. Bake at 200°C for 20 minutes, then reduce to 180°C for 40 minutes.
6. Cool 30 minutes before slicing. Serve with vanilla ice cream.

title: Banana Bread
ingredients: 3 very ripe bananas, 80g melted butter, 150g sugar, 2 eggs, 1 tsp vanilla, 200g plain flour, 1 tsp baking soda, ½ tsp salt, 100g walnuts (optional)
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 175°C. Grease a loaf tin.
2. Mash bananas with a fork until almost smooth.
3. Stir in melted butter, then sugar, eggs, and vanilla.
4. Fold in flour, baking soda, and salt until just combined.
5. Fold in walnuts if using.
6. Pour into tin and bake 60–65 minutes. A skewer should come out clean. Cool before slicing.

title: Fresh Lemonade
ingredients: 6 lemons (juiced), 150g caster sugar, 1L cold water, ice, fresh mint, lemon slices
instructions:
1. Make simple syrup: dissolve sugar in ½ cup hot water, cool.
2. Juice all lemons — you need about 240ml juice.
3. Combine lemon juice, simple syrup, and cold water in a pitcher.
4. Stir well and taste — adjust sweetness or sourness.
5. Fill glasses with ice, pour lemonade over.
6. Garnish with fresh mint and a lemon slice.

title: Mango Lassi
ingredients: 2 ripe mangoes, 300ml plain yogurt, 100ml milk, 2 tbsp honey, ½ tsp ground cardamom, pinch of saffron (optional), ice, mint to garnish
instructions:
1. Peel and dice mangoes.
2. Blend mango, yogurt, milk, honey, and cardamom until smooth.
3. Add a few ice cubes and blend again until frothy.
4. Taste and adjust sweetness.
5. Pour into tall glasses.
6. Garnish with a mint sprig and a pinch of cardamom. Serve cold.

title: Masala Chai
ingredients: 4 cups water, 2 cups whole milk, 3 tbsp loose black tea, 4 green cardamom pods, 2 cloves, 1 cinnamon stick, ¼ tsp ground ginger, 4 tbsp sugar
instructions:
1. Lightly crush cardamom pods and cloves in a mortar.
2. Bring water to boil with cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, and ginger.
3. Simmer spices 5 minutes until fragrant.
4. Add loose tea and simmer 3 more minutes.
5. Add milk and sugar. Bring back to a boil, then simmer 2 minutes.
6. Strain into cups and serve hot.

title: Classic Sangria
ingredients: 750ml red wine, 200ml orange juice, 60ml brandy, 2 tbsp sugar, 1 orange (sliced), 1 lemon (sliced), 1 apple (diced), 200ml sparkling water, ice, cinnamon stick
instructions:
1. Stir sugar into orange juice until dissolved.
2. Combine wine, orange juice, and brandy in a large pitcher.
3. Add sliced orange, lemon, diced apple, and cinnamon stick.
4. Stir and refrigerate at least 2 hours for flavors to develop.
5. Just before serving, add sparkling water and ice.
6. Pour into glasses ensuring fruit is included in each glass.

title: Classic Guacamole
ingredients: 3 ripe avocados, 1 lime (juiced), 1 small red onion (finely diced), 1 jalapeño (minced), 2 cloves garlic, handful fresh cilantro, ½ tsp salt, 1 ripe tomato (diced, seeds removed)
instructions:
1. Halve avocados, remove pits, and scoop flesh into a bowl.
2. Mash with a fork to desired texture (chunky or smooth).
3. Immediately add lime juice and stir to prevent browning.
4. Fold in red onion, jalapeño, garlic, and cilantro.
5. Gently stir in diced tomato.
6. Season with salt. Taste and adjust lime or jalapeño. Serve with tortilla chips.

title: Homemade Trail Mix
ingredients: 1 cup almonds, 1 cup cashews, ½ cup pumpkin seeds, ½ cup dried cranberries, ½ cup dark chocolate chips, ¼ cup coconut flakes, 1 tbsp honey, 1 tbsp coconut oil, 1 tsp cinnamon, pinch of salt
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 160°C. Line a baking sheet with parchment.
2. Toss almonds, cashews, and pumpkin seeds with melted coconut oil, honey, cinnamon, and salt.
3. Spread on sheet and bake 12 minutes, stirring halfway.
4. Cool completely.
5. Mix in dried cranberries, chocolate chips, and coconut flakes.
6. Store in an airtight jar for up to 2 weeks.

title: Chocolate Energy Balls
ingredients: 1 cup rolled oats, ½ cup peanut butter, ¼ cup honey, ½ cup dark chocolate chips, ¼ cup flaxseeds, 1 tsp vanilla extract, pinch of salt, desiccated coconut for rolling
instructions:
1. Combine oats, peanut butter, honey, chocolate chips, flaxseeds, vanilla, and salt in a bowl.
2. Stir until everything is well combined.
3. Refrigerate mixture for 30 minutes until firm enough to roll.
4. Roll tablespoon-sized portions into balls.
5. Coat in desiccated coconut.
6. Store in the fridge in an airtight container for up to 1 week.

title: Caramel Popcorn
ingredients: ⅓ cup popcorn kernels, 200g brown sugar, 100g butter, 60ml golden syrup, ½ tsp baking soda, 1 tsp vanilla extract, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp salt
instructions:
1. Pop kernels in oil in a large pot. Transfer to a very large bowl.
2. Preheat oven to 120°C. Line two baking sheets with parchment.
3. Melt butter in a saucepan. Add sugar and syrup, bring to boil.
4. Boil without stirring for 4 minutes until caramel forms.
5. Remove from heat. Stir in baking soda and vanilla — it will foam.
6. Pour over popcorn, toss quickly to coat. Spread on sheets, bake 45 minutes, stirring every 15 min.
